title: triticum triunciale subsp. kotschyi
intro: Triticum triunciale subsp. kotschyi, also known as wild einkorn, is a type of ancient wheat that is native to the Near East and is characterized by its small, three-grained spikelet. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its potential to treat digestive issues, infections, and inflammation. The health benefits of wild einkorn include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and provide antioxidants, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Wild einkorn can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, or it can be consumed as a food source, providing essential nutrients and fiber.
